symptoms.symptom_name: Complete cessation of oral fluid intake for ~48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Two days of zero intake is a direct cause of severe extracellular volume depletion.
Prolonged fluid deprivation drives renal hypoperfusion and pre-renal AKI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-syncope on sitting/standing; bedbound, unable to stand safely

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural presyncope is a hallmark of orthostatic hypotension from volume depletion.
Symptomatic orthostasis with tachycardia signals compensated shock at risk of decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Markedly reduced urine output with dry mouth

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Oliguria and dry mucous membranes reflect significant fluid deficit.
Falling urine output on a hypovolaemic background flags impending or established AKI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Racing heartbeat, ~110 bpm on a consumer wearable

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory sinus tachycardia to maintain cardiac output in volume depletion; patient-obtainable but unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deliberate cessation of self-care with expressed sense of no point sustaining her body (passive self-neglect)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute adjustment / existential crisis driving self-neglect
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The behavioural driver of the physical decline; also a mental-health risk requiring its own assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic hypotension (BP 90/55 lying, dropping to 80/50 on sitting) with resting tachycardia HR 112

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective postural blood-pressure drop with tachycardia — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory compromise from the reported near-syncope, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed capillary refill (4 seconds), decreased skin turgor, dry mucous membranes on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral perfusion and hydration signs confirming significant volume depletion — clinician-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum urea, electrolytes and creatinine (U&E) for acute kidney injury

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: U&E is required to confirm/exclude AKI and guide rehydration — a hospital/GP blood test, never available in real time via chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together — no intake 48h, postural near-syncope, oliguria/dry mouth and compensatory tachycardia — these patient-reportable features establish a time-critical volume-depletion emergency without any clinician measurement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To confirm/exclude acute kidney injury and electrolyte derangement and guide IV rehydration — obtained on urgent in-person assessment, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Point-of-care and formal orthostatic blood pressure and heart rate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objectively quantifies the postural drop and haemodynamic compromise the AI can only infer from reported near-syncope.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and infection screen (septic screen if indicated)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude occult infection/sepsis masked by apathy and tachycardia.
